items.q: What does Kant's categorical imperative require?
items.correct: Acting only on maxims you could will to be universal laws, and treating people as ends in themselves, never merely as means.
items.misconception: Treat others how you would like to be treated.
items.random_wrong: Do whatever your conscience tells you to do.
items.why: The Golden Rule depends on your personal preferences (Kant explicitly rejected it). The categorical imperative tests universalisability — whether the maxim can coherently be a law for everyone.

items.q: What do Christians mean by resurrection, and how does it differ from reincarnation?
items.correct: Resurrection is the once-for-all bodily raising to eternal life at the end of time (modelled on Christ's resurrection); reincarnation is the soul's repeated rebirth into new bodies. They are distinct doctrines from different religious traditions.
items.misconception: Resurrection and reincarnation are basically the same idea of life after death.
items.random_wrong: Christians believe everyone is reincarnated until they accept Jesus.
items.why: Christianity affirms one bodily resurrection, not cyclical rebirth. Students flatten different afterlife concepts into a single 'life after death' idea, missing the very different metaphysics involved.

items.q: What does Buddhism teach about suffering?
items.correct: All existence involves dukkha (suffering/unsatisfactoriness), caused by tanha (craving and attachment); ending craving leads to liberation from suffering.
items.misconception: Buddhism teaches that life is nothing but pain and misery.
items.random_wrong: Buddhism teaches that suffering is caused by demons.
items.why: Dukkha is broader than pain — it includes dissatisfaction and impermanence. Buddhism doesn't say life is misery; it says even pleasure carries inherent unsatisfactoriness because it ends.

items.q: Whose view of the soul as the body's form did Aquinas adopt?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Aristotle. “Plato” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which Buddhist doctrine denies a permanent soul?
items.why: Correct answer: Anatta. “Atman” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many traditional skandhas (aggregates) does Buddhism teach?
items.why: The real figure is 5. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
